question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user, by crafting a stream file where getConsensusStart() returns a value that causes arithmetic overflow when computing latency (streamFile.getConsensusStart() - last.getConsensusStart()), cause onVerified() to record a corrupted metric and potentially crash the metric recording, disrupting the gossip pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a stream file where getConsensusEnd() returns a value that causes Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) to throw a DateTimeException (e.g., nanosecond value out of range), cause onVerified() to throw an unchecked exception that prevents lastStreamFile from being updated, stalling all future transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing streamFileNotifier.verified() to throw an unchecked exception, prevent lastStreamFile from being updated in onVerified(), causing the downloader to repeatedly attempt to process the same stream file and never advance to subsequent files containing new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFile.copy().clear() is called to cache a copy in lastStreamFile, by crafting a stream file whose copy() method returns a reference to the original object, causing bytes and items to remain in memory and potentially corrupting the state used for subsequent hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing importerProperties.getStartBlockNumber() to return a very large value when lastStreamFile is empty, force setStreamFileIndex() to assign an extremely high index to the first stream file, causing downstream processing to skip or misorder all subsequent transaction blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setStreamFileIndex() uses lastStreamFile.get().map(StreamFile::getIndex).map(v -> v + 1) without overflow protection, by causing the index to reach Long.MAX_VALUE and overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, corrupting the block ordering and causing transactions to be misattributed or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() to throw a SignatureVerificationException, exploit the fact that downloadNextBatch() only logs a warning (log.warn) and returns normally, causing the entire batch of transactions to be silently dropped without any retry or alerting mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a crafted demo-network response where sigFilesMap is always empty, exploit the DEMO network check to trigger downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false), permanently halting the downloader and preventing all future transaction gossip on that instance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user, by triggering the endDate check in verifySignatures() or the demo-network check in downloadNextBatch() to set downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false), permanently disable the downloader via shouldDownload() returning false, causing all subsequent transactions to never be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStreamFileSignatureMultiMap()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a large number of signature files with the same StreamFilename key but different node identities, exploit the TreeMultimap's STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR (which uses Ordering.arbitrary()) to cause non-deterministic ordering that consistently places malicious signatures before legitimate ones, influencing which data file is downloaded first?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user, by crafting signatures where getNode().getNodeId() returns null, cause a NullPointerException in the Collectors.mapping() call inside statusMap(), which propagates as an unchecked exception through verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() and causes the entire batch to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that statusMap() removes CONSENSUS_REACHED entries before returning, causing the logged status map to hide which nodes actually reached consensus, making it impossible to detect a targeted suppression attack where specific transactions are being excluded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by causing Utility.archiveFile() to throw a FileOperationException when isWriteFiles() is true, exploit the catch block in verifySignatures() to skip the current node and try the next, but if all nodes fail archiving, return false and permanently drop the transactions without any retry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a stream file where getDataFilename() returns a filename that resolves to a different file than expected (e.g., via path traversal in StreamFilename.from()), cause the downloader to fetch and verify an attacker-controlled file, accepting it as a legitimate stream file and suppressing the real transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, by injecting a signature file with a StreamFilename that causes a NullPointerException in the TreeMultimap's natural ordering comparator, cause the collect() operation to fail and the entire batch of signatures to be lost, preventing all transactions from being gossiped?
